summary: Worktree-isolated Lane-V verification of the B/C/D residual-fix commits (atomic manifest, merge-tree determinism, cutover rerun) — re-derive GO/NITS/FAIL with mutation non-vacuity

verdicts.fresh_test_result: `env -u GIT_INDEX_FILE PYTHONPATH=$PWD .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_threeway_cursor_backfill.py -q` -> 12 passed in 0.03s. `... test_threeway_cutover.py -q` -> 9 passed in 7.34s. Both together -> 21 passed in 6.74s. COUNT RECONCILED: brief/commit say cursor_backfill 10/0 — that was the count AT commit 1aba208b (pre-fix file had 6 def test_, the diff added 4 -> 10). The worktree is at HEAD=54144d98 (ADR-049, the child commit), which added 2 more tests (test_iso_to_seq_map_rejects_non_iso_cursor, test_archived_seq_map_returns_manifest_iso_to_seq) -> 12 now. Verified via `git diff 1aba208b..HEAD -- threeway/cursor_backfill.py`: ADR-049 touched ONLY iso_to_seq_map + added archived_seq_map; it did NOT alter any ADR-047 code (_atomic_write_text/_load_manifest/the two reads/the atomic write all byte-identical to 1aba208b). Cutover-message said 14 (cutover+legacy-cursor); the cutover file alone is 9 — unaffected. NON-VACUOUS RED: swapped in 1aba208b~1:threeway/cursor_backfill.py (pre-fix, confirmed has no _load_manifest/_atomic_write_text/CursorBackfillManifestError/os.replace) and ran the 4 new tests -> 4 failed (AttributeError: module has no attribute 'CursorBackfillManifestError' for the 3 typed-error tests; the crash test also fails). The mutation probes below are the stronger load-bearing proof.

mutations.name: (a) _load_manifest -> bare json.loads (return json.loads(path.read_text()), no try/except, no validation)
mutations.detail: test_corrupt_manifest_backfill_raises_clear_typed_error AND test_corrupt_manifest_restore_raises_clear_typed_error both FAILED: raised raw json.decoder.JSONDecodeError ('Unterminated string starting at: line 6 column 21') on the truncated manifest instead of cb.CursorBackfillManifestError. 2 failed in 0.04s. Reverted.

mutations.name: (b) remove the required-key (missing/!dict) check loop in _load_manifest
mutations.detail: test_keyincomplete_manifest_raises_clear_typed_error FAILED: a schema-valid manifest with iso_to_seq deleted reached the resume branch `obj['iso_to_seq'].items()` -> raised bare KeyError: 'iso_to_seq' at cursor_backfill.py:133 instead of the typed error. 1 failed in 0.03s. Reverted.

mutations.name: (c) _atomic_write_text -> plain path.write_text(text), no tmp + no os.replace
mutations.detail: test_crash_during_atomic_manifest_write_leaves_no_committed_manifest_and_retry_resumes FAILED: monkeypatched os.replace is never called, so the simulated RuntimeError crash never fires -> pytest 'Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class RuntimeError>' at line 143. Proves the test genuinely depends on os.replace being the commit point. 1 failed in 0.02s. Reverted. Worktree confirmed clean (git diff empty) after all 3 reverts.

verdicts.correctness: Success path behavior-identical: PYTHONPATH run confirmed cb._load_manifest(p) == json.loads(p.read_text()) == the original payload dict for a valid atomically-written manifest; no leftover .tmp turd. CursorBackfillManifestError subclasses ValueError: issubclass(...) -> True (preserves the prior restore_from_manifest schema-mismatch ValueError contract). No auto-re-derive on corruption: _load_manifest raises the typed error rather than re-deriving; backfill's resume branch (cursor_backfill.py:135-139) re-APPLIES the archived scalar iso_to_seq map and returns — it never re-derives ISO->seq from possibly-already-scalar seen/*.txt, so a corrupt manifest cannot silently clobber a scalar cursor. The atomic write runs BEFORE any cursor rewrite (line 149 before 157-158), so a crash leaves seen/*.txt still ISO -> retry resumes via the fresh-archive branch. Cutover step 5b (cutover.py:176) calls backfill OUTSIDE the _teardown try, comment 'INTENTIONALLY not torn down ... retry resumes cheaply' — the ADR-047 atomic write is exactly what makes that invariant hold.
verdicts.rule13: Clean. Sweep `git grep -n json.loads -- threeway/*.py`: the only manifest reader is cursor_backfill.py:46 (now inside _load_manifest; both readers backfill-resume + restore_from_manifest + archived_seq_map all route through it). Other json.loads are refstore.py:206/216 and store.py:60 — these read EVENT-BUS blobs (a different artifact) and are already drop-not-raise guarded (except (ValueError,KeyError,TypeError): continue, ADR-046). Non-atomic-write sweep `git grep -nE '\.write_text|\.write_bytes|json.dump' -- threeway/*.py`: the migration manifest write is the only load-bearing migration artifact and it is now atomic (_atomic_write_text tmp+os.replace); store.py:49-50 already uses tmp.replace(path); keys_bootstrap/seen-*.txt writes are not load-bearing-migration artifacts (seen/*.txt stay ISO until the manifest commits, by design). No twin hole.
verdicts.notes: Files: /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/threeway/cursor_backfill.py, /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/tests/unit/test_threeway_cursor_backfill.py, /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/DECISIONS.md (ADR-047 entry), /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/docs/REMEDIATION-INVENTORY.md (row threeway-cursor-backfill-manifest-nonatomic, status=fixed). Worktree HEAD=54144d986 (ADR-049, child of 1aba208b); verified 1aba208b's ADR-047 code is byte-identical at HEAD via git diff, so mutating-at-HEAD is a valid proxy. Inventory row says 'Lane-V pending' and the ADR-047 DECISIONS entry says 'Independent Lane-V pending' — this verification discharges that (coordinator may reconcile open->verified / annotate the Lane-V GO). No lock held in scope. No defects found.

verdicts.fresh_test_result: tests/unit/test_threeway_gitcas.py: 20 passed in 3.15s (fresh run at HEAD 54144d98, fix at 219111fa). Named pins all present + green: test_merge_tree_is_deterministic_under_ambient_merge_config (line 287), test_merge_tree_clean_returns_tree_and_true (226), test_merge_tree_conflict_returns_false_even_though_git_prints_a_tree (232) -> 3 passed. Broader: full threeway slice `pytest tests/unit -k threeway` -> 341 passed, 2801 deselected (107s); commit body cited 338, +3 from the two ADR-049 follow-on commits now on HEAD — consistent.

mutations.name: (a) remove `-c merge.renames=true` pin from merge_tree
mutations.detail: Removed the merge.renames=true `-c` line (renameLimit + diff.algorithm pins kept). test_merge_tree_is_deterministic_under_ambient_merge_config went RED with EXACTLY the commit-cited divergence: AssertionError: merge_tree non-deterministic under ambient merge.renames: ('9bb1321d...e81bf5', True) != ('a85642ec...edfbe', False). Full suite under mutation = 1 failed, 19 passed -- the determinism test is the ONLY test that catches this guard, and it does. Non-vacuous RED confirmed; reverted, tree clean.

mutations.name: (b) flip pin value to `merge.renames=false`
mutations.detail: NOTE not a defect: with `false` PINNED, determinism test STILL PASSES (3 pins green) -- a pinned `false` is also host-independent (both ambient runs see the identical pinned value). The test's contract is DETERMINISM (pin present), not direction (renames ON). It catches an ABSENT pin (host variance), not a wrong VALUE. The chosen value `true` (git default; restores rename-following) is justified in ADR-048 but is not independently asserted by any test. Coverage note, not blocking. Reverted.

verdicts.correctness: CONFIRMED the `-c` pins did NOT change merge SEMANTICS, only removed host-config variance. The two existing semantics pins (test_merge_tree_clean_returns_tree_and_true, test_merge_tree_conflict_returns_false_even_though_git_prints_a_tree) PASS on the real fix (3 passed) AND stay GREEN under mutation (a) (19 passed incl. both). They test the feat-add-clean and base.txt-modify/modify-conflict cases — neither involves a rename, so the rename/renameLimit/diff.algorithm pins are inert for them; exit-code-driven `clean` is unchanged. The pins set canonical values (rename detection ON = git default with generous limit; histogram = deterministic widely-available diff) that match git defaults for the no-ambient-config case, so behavior is identical absent ambient config.
verdicts.rule13: SWEEP CLEAN. Enumerated every git subcommand in gitcas.py: only `merge-tree` is merge-algorithm-sensitive (now pinned). The one other algorithm-config-touching call is `changed_paths` (gitcas.py:51-53, `git diff --name-only`, used by predicate.py:107 -> effective_tier -> tier-escalation REJECT at predicate.py:118) — config-sensitive in PRINCIPLE (diff.renames/diff.algorithm), but empirically VERIFIED determinism-SAFE: `--name-only` output is identical under `diff.renames=true` vs `false` (rename detection only changes the rename HEADER, not the path enumeration — both old+new path are listed either way; diff.algorithm affects hunk content not file list). So no second seat-disagreement vector. gate.py:201/204 (the live caller: merge_tree -> commit_tree -> integration_sha equality + main-tip CAS) is now fully deterministic: tree via the config-pinned merge_tree, commit identity via _DET_ENV. All remaining calls are content-addressed (hash-object/cat-file), identity-pinned (commit-tree/commit_on via _DET_ENV), or structural plumbing (ref/index/tree, hardcoded mode 100644 in build_tree_with) with no merge/diff algorithm. No unpinned config-sensitive determinism sibling left open.
verdicts.notes: RESIDUAL (do-not-fix, correctly scoped out by the commit/ADR-048): cross-git-VERSION determinism — the merge engine itself can change across major git releases; `-c` flags cannot pin that. Mitigation is a deployment-layer git-version floor, documented not code-enforced. Acknowledged in commit body + DECISIONS.md ADR-048 + inventory row. SCOPE: diff touches exactly threeway/gitcas.py, tests/unit/test_threeway_gitcas.py, DECISIONS.md (ADR-048 added, prior entries untouched), docs/REMEDIATION-INVENTORY.md (1 row added). NO .claude/settings.json. Working tree confirmed clean after all mutations reverted (git status --porcelain empty). I made no edits/commits — read/run/mutation-revert only. COVERAGE NIT (non-blocking, see mutation b): the merge.renames VALUE direction (true vs false) is not independently asserted by a test; only pin-presence/determinism is. The chosen `true` is sound (git default) and ADR-justified, so no FAIL — flagging for the implementer's awareness only. Files: /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/.claude/worktrees/wf_5906f5e7-9d5-2/threeway/gitcas.py, /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/.claude/worktrees/wf_5906f5e7-9d5-2/tests/unit/test_threeway_gitcas.py, /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/.claude/worktrees/wf_5906f5e7-9d5-2/threeway/gate.py, /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/.claude/worktrees/wf_5906f5e7-9d5-2/threeway/predicate.py

mutations.name: (a) cutover step-4 manifest branch off (if False and _manifest_path...exists())
mutations.detail: test_force_rerun_does_not_overadvance_cursor_ref_from_scalar_seen -> FAILED. With only the manifest branch off, layer-(2) non-ISO guard catches the scalar and raises CursorBackfillManifestError('director2' cursor '5' not ISO) -> pin RED (the complementary defense-in-depth fires). To prove the ORIGINAL silent over-advance (not just the raise), I ALSO disabled layer-(2): the pin then reproduced the EXACT documented defect -> AssertionError: assert 5 == 3 (cursor_seq('director') over-advanced 3->5). Non-vacuity confirmed end-to-end; both reverted.

mutations.name: (b) remove non-ISO guard in iso_to_seq_map
mutations.detail: test_iso_to_seq_map_rejects_non_iso_cursor -> FAILED: 'DID NOT RAISE CursorBackfillManifestError' (cursor_backfill.py:177). Guard reverted, worktree clean.

verdicts.correctness: First-run behavior unchanged: manifest absent -> else-branch iso_to_seq_map(carrier_names,_read_iso_cursors(...)) is byte-identical to pre-fix; existing cutover first-run tests pass. advance_cursor (threeway/refstore.py) NOT touched by this commit (git show -- refstore.py empty) -> monotonic-forward contract preserved. Re-run uses archived ISO-derived seq: archived_seq_map returns dict(_load_manifest[...]['iso_to_seq']) = the first-run ISO-derived map (verified by test_archived_seq_map_returns_manifest_iso_to_seq + the 3->3 stays-at-true-seq assertion). _ISO regex verified to accept colon-form ISO ('2026-06-17T12:00:00Z'->True), map empty->seq0, reject scalars ('3'/'5'->False) -> no false-rejection of valid first-run cursors.
verdicts.rule13: Both audit-flagged gaps confirmed present in code, NOT addressed by this commit, and correctly NOT filed inline (track as SEPARATE open rows): (1) ORDERING-CONGRUENCE — three total-order derivations remain divergent: legacy_projector._EVENT_NAME_RE (legacy_projector.py:23, captures 'ts') vs cursor_backfill._TS (cursor_backfill.py:15) vs the numeric index in iso_to_seq_map; diff touches neither (the _TS line at diff:203 is unchanged context adjacent to the new _ISO line). (2) seen-filename->seat-KEY mapping — {p.stem:...} at cutover.py:54 and cursor_backfill.py:142 (stem collisions) + seq_map.get(seat,0) at cutover.py:163 silently resetting a missing real seat to 0; diff:269 shows seq_map.get as unchanged context only. Confirmed via git show: no +/- change to either logic. Inventory grep finds no existing row for either -> should be filed separately. Correctly out of scope; not fixed (per brief, do NOT fix).
verdicts.notes: Scope matches brief: 7 files (threeway/cutover.py, threeway/cursor_backfill.py, both tests, DECISIONS.md ADR-049, docs/REMEDIATION-INVENTORY.md row threeway-cutover-rerun-scalar-cursor-overadvance, ARCHITECTURE.md); NO .claude/settings.json. ARCHITECTURE.md re-anchors all CORRECT vs HEAD code: def run_gate -> threeway/gate.py:158; def cas_create_or_update_ref -> threeway/gitcas.py:185; def preflight_bus_init -> threeway/gitcas.py:245. Two complementary fix layers verified: (1) source seq-map from archived manifest on re-run, (2) reject non-ISO cursors loudly. The two-layer interaction is sound — even with layer-1 bypassed, layer-2 makes the re-run fail-SAFE (raise->teardown) rather than silently over-advance (mutation-confirmed both ways). No lock held / no lock release implicated. No FAIL or NITS.
